description: The image displays three panels of satellite imagery, serving as an example of image co-registration in Interferometric Synthetic Aperture Radar (InSAR) processing. The leftmost panel shows a grayscale Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) amplitude image, depicting ground reflectivity patterns with brighter tones indicating urban or artificial surfaces and darker tones representing vegetated or water areas, illustrating features like roads and buildings. The middle and right panels are coloured interferograms, each exhibiting distinct wave-like fringe patterns in hues of red, orange, yellow, green, and blue against a textured grey background. These fringes represent the interferometric phase, derived from the phase difference between two co-registered SAR acquisitions, sensitive to topography and deformation. The visual difference in fringe patterns between the middle and right panels suggests either different interferometric pairs or distinct stages of phase processing. This type of imagery is typically visually inspected by SAR operators before extensive InSAR analysis.
